plan.title: Improve Redis Tool: Tests-First, Then Iterate (v2 — Review Feedback Applied)
plan.description: Add comprehensive test coverage and fix 8 identified issues in tools/redis.ts using a tests-first approach
plan.overview: This plan implements Option 4 (Tests-First, Then Iterate) for improving tools/redis.ts. Phase 1 creates test infrastructure and writes tests for all exported pure functions and the RedisDriver class. A prerequisite step installs ioredis as a devDependency for TypeScript types. Phase 2 fixes 7 issues incrementally (TDD style: test first, then fix). Phase 3 verifies all tests pass and TypeScript compiles cleanly. The plan uses bun test (built-in, no deps needed) and creates tools/redis.test.ts from scratch. Each fix in Phase 2 has its own test case written first before the implementation change. Integration tests with a real Redis server are out of scope but noted as future work.

checkpoints.title: Create Test Infrastructure and Write Tests for Pure Functions
checkpoints.description: Create tools/redis.test.ts with bun test. Write tests for all 7 exported pure functions: extractRedisIdentifier, sanitizeConnectionString, parseConnectionString, formatValue, formatKeyValueTable, formatList, formatResult. Add a 'test:redis' script to package.json (do NOT overwrite the existing 'test' script). Include edge cases: NaN, Infinity, Symbol in formatValue; pipe characters in keys for formatKeyValueTable; IPv6 addresses in parseConnectionString.

acceptance_criteria.description: tools/redis.test.ts exists and contains test blocks for all 7 exported pure functions
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: ls tools/redis.test.ts; grep for 'describe' or 'test' for each of the 7 functions

acceptance_criteria.description: extractRedisIdentifier tests: valid URL returns hostname:port/db, invalid URL returns raw string, URL with no port defaults to 6379, URL with no db defaults to 0
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'extractRedisIdentifier'; assert all 4 scenarios pass

acceptance_criteria.description: sanitizeConnectionString tests: URL with password redacts it to ****, non-URL string with password-like pattern is masked, URL without password is unchanged, rediss:// scheme is also handled
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'sanitizeConnectionString'; assert all 4 scenarios pass including rediss://

acceptance_criteria.description: parseConnectionString tests: full URL returns correct host/port/password/db, minimal URL returns defaults (localhost/6379/undefined/0), URL with db path parses db number correctly, IPv6 address in URL is parsed correctly
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'parseConnectionString'; assert all 4 scenarios pass including IPv6 like redis://[::1]:6379/0

acceptance_criteria.description: formatValue tests: null/undefined returns NULL, Buffer returns utf-8 string, object returns JSON stringified, string returns itself, number returns stringified number, NaN returns 'NaN', Infinity returns 'Infinity', Symbol returns its string representation
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'formatValue'; assert all 8 scenarios pass

acceptance_criteria.description: formatKeyValueTable tests: empty array returns _(empty)_, single entry produces valid markdown table with header/separator/row, multiple entries produce correct table, long values are truncated at 200 chars, pipe characters in keys/values are escaped with backslash
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'formatKeyValueTable'; assert all 5 scenarios pass including pipe chars in keys like 'key|name'

acceptance_criteria.description: formatList tests: empty array returns _(empty)_, single item returns numbered list, multiple items return correctly numbered list
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'formatList'; assert all 3 scenarios pass

acceptance_criteria.description: formatResult tests: returns markdown bold label followed by formatted value
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'formatResult'; assert output matches **label:** value pattern

acceptance_criteria.description: package.json has a new 'test:redis' script that runs 'bun test tools/redis.test.ts'; the existing 'test' script is NOT modified
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect package.json scripts; assert 'test:redis' exists with 'bun test tools/redis.test.ts' and 'test' still contains 'npx ts-node'

security_concerns.description: Tests for sanitizeConnectionString may inadvertently log real-looking passwords if test data resembles production secrets
security_concerns.mitigation: Use clearly fake test credentials (e.g., 'redis://user:test123@host:6379') that are obviously not real

checkpoints.title: Install ioredis as DevDependency for TypeScript Types
checkpoints.description: ioredis is dynamically imported at runtime but is NOT in package.json. TypeScript cannot resolve import('ioredis') types without it. Install ioredis as a devDependency so that CP-09 (replace any types) can use proper ioredis type definitions. This is a prerequisite for all TypeScript type improvements.

acceptance_criteria.description: ioredis is listed in devDependencies in package.json
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect package.json devDependencies; assert 'ioredis' is present

acceptance_criteria.description: TypeScript can resolve import('ioredis') without errors
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: npx tsc --noEmit --strict --moduleResolution node --module esnext -e 'const x: typeof import("ioredis") = null;' 2>&1; assert exit code 0

security_concerns.description: Adding ioredis as a dependency introduces a new supply chain attack surface
security_concerns.mitigation: Pin the ioredis version to a known-good release; run 'bun add -d ioredis@5.4.1' or latest stable; review the package for suspicious postinstall scripts

checkpoints.title: Write Tests for RedisDriver Class with Mocked ioredis Client
checkpoints.description: Write comprehensive tests for the RedisDriver class using Bun.mock.module('ioredis', ...) to mock the ioredis module. Test connect(), execute() for all action types, close(), error handling paths (ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEOUT, NOAUTH, WRONGTYPE, unknown action, ioredis-not-installed), and retry logic. Note: AC-03-05 (keys warning test) will be modified in CP-04 when the KEYS '' bug is fixed.

acceptance_criteria.description: Mocking strategy uses Bun.mock.module('ioredis', ...) to provide a fake ioredis default export with all needed methods (connect, get, set, keys, mset, quit, etc.)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.test.ts; assert Bun.mock.module('ioredis', ...) is used for mocking

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.connect() with valid connection string creates client and calls connect() on it
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver connect'; assert client.connect was called

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.connect() when ioredis is not available throws error mentioning 'bun add ioredis'
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver connect.*not installed'; assert error message contains 'bun add ioredis'

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.execute('get', { key }) returns formatted result with value from mocked client.get()
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver execute get'; assert output contains '**Value:**'

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.execute('keys', { pattern }) with pattern='*' triggers console.warn about blocking (will be refined in CP-04)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver execute keys.*warning'; assert console.warn was called

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.execute('mset', { keyValues }) calls client.mset() with flattened key-value pairs
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver execute mset'; assert client.mset was called with correct args

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.execute() with unknown action throws error listing supported actions
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver execute unknown'; assert error message contains 'Unknown action'

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.execute() handles ECONNREFUSED error and throws user-friendly message mentioning 'Connection refused'
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver execute.*ECONNREFUSED'; assert error mentions 'Connection refused'

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.execute() handles ETIMEOUT error and throws user-friendly message mentioning 'timed out'
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver execute.*ETIMEOUT'; assert error mentions 'timed out' or 'timeout'

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.execute() handles NOAUTH error and throws user-friendly message mentioning 'Authentication failed'
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver execute.*NOAUTH'; assert error mentions 'Authentication failed'

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.execute() handles WRONGTYPE error and throws user-friendly message mentioning 'wrong kind of value'
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver execute.*WRONGTYPE'; assert error mentions 'wrong kind'

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.close() calls client.quit() and sets client to null
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver close'; assert client.quit was called and client is null

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.execute() returns RedisResult with correct output, metadata (action, key, connection, duration)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver execute.*metadata'; assert result.metadata has action, key, connection, duration fields

acceptance_criteria.description: Retry strategy test: client is created with retryStrategy that returns increasing delays and stops after 3 attempts
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver retry strategy'; assert retryStrategy(1) returns 200, retryStrategy(4) returns null

security_concerns.description: Mocked tests may not catch real ioredis API mismatches if the mock doesn't match actual ioredis behavior
security_concerns.mitigation: Use type assertions that match ioredis method signatures; document that integration tests with real Redis are needed for full coverage

checkpoints.title: Fix KEYS '' Warning (Issue #7) — Only Warn for '*', Not ''
checkpoints.description: The current code at line 196 warns for both pattern === '*' and pattern === ''. An empty pattern '' is not the same as '*' — it matches only keys with empty string names. Fix the condition to only warn for '*'. Write the test case first (TDD). Also update the test written in CP-03 AC-03-05 to reflect the corrected behavior.

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('keys', { pattern: '' }) does NOT trigger console.warn
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'keys empty pattern no warning'; assert console.warn was NOT called

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('keys', { pattern: '*' }) still triggers console.warn
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'keys star pattern warns'; assert console.warn was called

acceptance_criteria.description: Source code fix: line 196 condition changed from (pattern === '*' || pattern === '') to (pattern === '*')
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.ts line 196; assert condition is pattern === '*' only

acceptance_criteria.description: CP-03 AC-03-05 test is updated to match the new behavior (empty pattern no longer warns)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'RedisDriver execute keys'; assert all keys-related tests pass

checkpoints.title: Fix startTime || Date.now() Dead Code (Issue #6)
checkpoints.description: At line 865, the error handler uses `startTime || Date.now()` for duration calculation. But `startTime` is always defined (set at line 833) and is a number, so the fallback is dead code. Remove the fallback to just use `startTime`. Write the test case first.

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: error path in tool.execute() returns duration that is a positive number, not NaN or 0, and is calculated from startTime
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'error duration uses startTime'; assert duration is a positive number and not NaN

acceptance_criteria.description: Source code fix: line 865 changed from `Date.now() - (startTime || Date.now())` to `Date.now() - startTime`
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.ts line 865; assert expression is 'Date.now() - startTime'

checkpoints.title: Gate TLS Warning to Fire Once Per Session (Issue #4)
checkpoints.description: The TLS warning at lines 826-831 (in the tool's execute function, not RedisDriver) fires on every non-TLS connection. This is noisy for repeated calls. Gate it with a module-level boolean flag so it only fires once per session. To avoid test isolation issues, expose a resetTlsWarningFlag() function that tests can call in beforeEach. Write the test case first.

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: first non-TLS connection triggers console.warn for TLS warning (test lives in the tool.execute test section)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'TLS warning first call'; assert console.warn was called

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: second non-TLS connection does NOT trigger console.warn for TLS warning (test lives in the tool.execute test section)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'TLS warning second call'; assert console.warn was called only once total

acceptance_criteria.description: Source code fix: add module-level let tlsWarningShown = false; gate the console.warn behind it; set to true after first fire; expose resetTlsWarningFlag() for test isolation
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.ts; assert tlsWarningShown flag exists, gates the warning, and resetTlsWarningFlag() is exported

acceptance_criteria.description: Test isolation: beforeEach calls resetTlsWarningFlag() so tests don't interfere with each other
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.test.ts; assert beforeEach or equivalent calls resetTlsWarningFlag()

checkpoints.title: Add Input Validation for Key, Pattern, and All User-Controlled Parameters (Issue #8, Expanded)
checkpoints.description: Add input validation in RedisDriver.execute() for all user-controlled parameters: key, newKey, pattern, field, member, element. Validate: reject null bytes, enforce max length (1024 chars for pattern, 512 chars for keys/fields/members/elements). Add size limits for bulk read operations: cap lrange at 1000 items, limit mget to 100 keys, limit keys to 10000 results. Write the test case first.

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('keys', { pattern: 'a'.repeat(1025) }) throws error about pattern too long (validation in RedisDriver.execute() keys case)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'keys pattern too long'; assert error mentions 'pattern' and '1024'

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('scan', { pattern: 'a\0b' }) throws error about null byte in pattern
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'scan pattern null byte'; assert error mentions 'null byte' or 'invalid'

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('keys', { pattern: 'valid:*' }) succeeds without validation error
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'keys valid pattern'; assert no validation error thrown

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('get', { key: 'key\0name' }) throws error about null byte in key
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'key null byte'; assert error mentions 'null byte' or 'invalid'

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('rename', { key: 'valid', newKey: 'x'.repeat(513) }) throws error about newKey too long
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'newKey too long'; assert error mentions 'newKey' and '512'

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('hget', { key: 'k', field: 'field\0name' }) throws error about null byte in field
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'field null byte'; assert error mentions 'null byte'

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('sismember', { key: 'k', member: 'mem\0ber' }) throws error about null byte in member
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'member null byte'; assert error mentions 'null byte'

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('lrem', { key: 'k', count: 1, element: 'el\0' }) throws error about null byte in element
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'element null byte'; assert error mentions 'null byte'

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('lrange', { key: 'k', start: 0, stop: 2000 }) caps stop to 999 (max 1000 items)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'lrange cap'; assert client.lrange was called with stop <= 999

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('mget', { keys: Array(101).fill('k') }) throws error about too many keys (max 100)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'mget too many keys'; assert error mentions '100' or 'too many'

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('keys', { pattern: '*' }) with mocked client returning 10001 keys caps output at 10000
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'keys result cap'; assert output mentions '10000' or truncation

acceptance_criteria.description: Source code fix: add input validation helper or inline checks in RedisDriver.execute() for all affected parameters before calling client
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.ts; assert validation exists for key, newKey, pattern, field, member, element in the relevant switch cases

security_concerns.description: Unvalidated inputs could be used for Redis command injection or denial-of-service via extremely long patterns/keys
security_concerns.mitigation: Reject null bytes in all string parameters; enforce max lengths (1024 for pattern, 512 for keys/fields/members/elements); cap bulk operation sizes

security_concerns.description: Bulk read operations (lrange, mget, keys) without size limits could cause OOM or excessive network transfer
security_concerns.mitigation: Cap lrange to 1000 items, mget to 100 keys, keys to 10000 results; document limits in error messages

checkpoints.title: Fix mset Type Coercion (Issue #3) — Preserve Type Info and Serialize Objects
checkpoints.description: At line 424, mset converts all values to String(v), losing type information (numbers, booleans, null). Fix to pass values as-is to ioredis, which handles type serialization correctly. For non-primitive values (objects, arrays), serialize via JSON.stringify before passing. Write the test case first.

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('mset', { keyValues: { a: 42, b: true, c: null } }) passes raw values to client.mset()
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'mset preserves types'; assert client.mset was called with 42, true, null (not '42', 'true', 'null')

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('mset', { keyValues: { a: 'hello' } }) still works with string values
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'mset string values'; assert client.mset was called with 'hello'

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: execute('mset', { keyValues: { a: { nested: true }, b: [1,2,3] } }) serializes objects/arrays via JSON.stringify before passing
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'mset object serialization'; assert client.mset was called with JSON stringified values for objects and arrays

acceptance_criteria.description: Source code fix: line 424 changed from kvPairs.push(k, String(v)) to kvPairs.push(k, typeof v === 'object' ? JSON.stringify(v) : v)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.ts line 424; assert String(v) is replaced with proper type-aware serialization

checkpoints.title: Replace any Types with Proper ioredis Types (Issue #2) — Distinct Types for Module vs Client
checkpoints.description: Replace `any` types throughout tools/redis.ts with proper ioredis types. Key changes: redisModule (line 7) should be typed as typeof import('ioredis') | null; RedisDriver.redis (module type) and RedisDriver.client (instance type) need DISTINCT types; error catch (line 623) should use unknown. Write type-level tests first.

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: TypeScript compilation passes with no 'any' type errors in tools/redis.ts
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun run tsc --noEmit --strict tools/redis.ts 2>&1; assert exit code 0

acceptance_criteria.description: redisModule variable typed as typeof import('ioredis') | null instead of any
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.ts line 7; assert type is not 'any'

acceptance_criteria.description: RedisDriver.redis (module reference) typed as typeof import('ioredis') | null; RedisDriver.client (instance) typed as import('ioredis').Redis | null — distinct types
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.ts lines 122-123; assert redis and client have different types (module vs instance)

acceptance_criteria.description: catch (error: any) on line 623 changed to catch (error: unknown) with proper type narrowing
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.ts line 623; assert type is 'unknown' not 'any'

acceptance_criteria.description: All function signatures using 'any' in formatValue, formatList, formatKeyValueTable, formatResult use proper types (unknown or generic)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: grep -n 'any' tools/redis.ts; assert no remaining 'any' types (or only unavoidable ones with justification)

security_concerns.description: Improper ioredis type usage could mask API incompatibilities if the type definitions don't match the actual runtime behavior
security_concerns.mitigation: Use the ioredis types from the installed package (ioredis ships its own types); verify with runtime tests

checkpoints.title: Refactor Error Handler to Use sanitizeConnectionString (Issue #5, Option A)
checkpoints.description: sanitizeConnectionString is exported but never called in the execution path. The error handler at lines 851-857 does its own inline redaction which only handles redis:// scheme, not rediss://. Refactor the error handler to use sanitizeConnectionString, which handles both schemes via URL parsing. Remove the inline regex. Write the test case first.

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: error handler output uses sanitizeConnectionString to redact credentials in error messages
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'error handler uses sanitizeConnectionString'; assert sanitizeConnectionString is called during error handling

acceptance_criteria.description: Test case written first: error with rediss:// connection string redacts password correctly (not just redis://)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts -t 'error handler rediss scheme'; assert rediss://user:pass@host is redacted to rediss://user:****@host

acceptance_criteria.description: Source code fix: error handler calls sanitizeConnectionString(connStr) instead of inline regex; inline regex at lines 855-857 is removed
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Inspect tools/redis.ts lines 851-857; assert sanitizeConnectionString is called and inline regex is removed

acceptance_criteria.description: All existing tests still pass after the change
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts; assert all tests pass

security_concerns.description: Error messages may leak Redis credentials if sanitization is incomplete or inconsistent
security_concerns.mitigation: Use sanitizeConnectionString which handles both redis:// and rediss:// schemes via URL parsing; verify with test cases for both schemes

checkpoints.title: Final Verification: All Tests Pass and TypeScript Compiles Cleanly
checkpoints.description: Run the full test suite and TypeScript compiler to confirm all changes are correct and no regressions were introduced. This is the final gate before handoff. Integration tests with a real Redis server are out of scope and should be documented as future work.

acceptance_criteria.description: All tests in tools/redis.test.ts pass (bun test exit code 0)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts; assert exit code 0 and all tests pass

acceptance_criteria.description: TypeScript compilation succeeds with no errors (tsc --noEmit exit code 0)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: npx tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json 2>&1; assert exit code 0

acceptance_criteria.description: Test output contains no unexpected console.warn or console.log calls beyond the test framework's own output (expected warnings from intentional tests are acceptable)
acceptance_criteria.verification_method: Run: bun test tools/redis.test.ts 2>&1; grep for 'console.warn' and 'console.log' in test output; assert only expected warnings (from tests that intentionally trigger warnings) are present

security_concerns.description: Integration tests with a real Redis server are not covered by this plan; unit tests with mocks may miss real-world edge cases
security_concerns.mitigation: Document the need for integration tests as future work; consider creating ADR-001 for test strategy
